items.title: Define the business question before picking a method
items.avoid: Running PCA or k-means because they are popular, without stating what structure you need.
items.prefer: Ask whether you need groups, compression, anomalies, or density—then choose the matching unsupervised family.
items.why: The paradigm is broad; method choice should follow the decision you need to support.
items.example: For fraud triage with rare events, start with anomaly detection scores; for marketing personas, clustering with reviewed features.

items.title: Make geometry explicit via preprocessing
items.avoid: Feeding raw mixed-scale features into distance-based clustering.
items.prefer: Standardize numeric features, encode categoricals consistently, and document the similarity notion.
items.why: Unsupervised results are defined by the metric space you impose.
items.example: Use `StandardScaler` before k-means; for text, cluster embedding vectors rather than raw token counts.

items.title: Validate discovered structure externally
items.avoid: Shipping cluster IDs to production based only on a high silhouette score.
items.prefer: Review cluster profiles, test stability across seeds/subsamples, and compare to any partial labels or KPIs.
items.why: Internal metrics cannot prove business usefulness.
items.example: Plot cluster centroids per feature, check if top decile of anomaly scores correlates with support escalations.
